All parties stipulate to Judge Pro tem James Fleming hearing today's motion. The court and parties present authorize the clerk to sign the Judge Pro Tem stipulation on their behalves.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

WHITE CAP CONSTRUCTION SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH-MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AS TO THE DEPOSITION OF SARB GILL

Defendant's Motion to Quash-Motion for Protective Order as to the Deposition of Sarb Gill is granted in part and otherwise denied, as follows:

1. The Motion to Quash the subpoena addressed to Deponent Sarb Gill is granted on grounds the subpoena is superfluous under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2080(a) and further has not been served on the Deponent.

2. The Motion for Protective Order is granted as to the employee file of Luis Perez on grounds Defendant has failed to serve the required Consumer Notice on the Defendant's former employee named Luis Perez who drove the truck involved in this action ("the correct Luis Perez") as required under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.240 and has failed to schedule the deposition at least 20 days after the issuance of the subpoena under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.270(c).

3. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the scheduling of the deposition of Sarb Gill and the last known address of the correct Luis Perez.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.
